title: Pandav Falls
tagline: Pandav Falls — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: The Pandav Falls is a waterfall in the Panna district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: The Pandav Falls is a waterfall in the Panna district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. About 30 metres (98 ft) high, it is located on a tributary of the Ken River, as it plunges over the falls to join it. Pandav Falls is located 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) from Panna and 35 kilometres (22 mi) from Khajuraho, close to Raneh Falls. The falls are named after the legendary Pandava brothers of the epic poem Mahabharata, who supposedly visited this area.

topPlaces.name: Gatha Falls
topPlaces.description: The Gatha Falls (or Gatha Seha) is a waterfall in Panna district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It is the 36th highest waterfall in India.

topPlaces.name: Jaruapur
topPlaces.description: Jaruapur is a village in Panna district of Madhya Pradesh state of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pandav Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pandav Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pandav Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jabalpur Airport (173 km) and the nearest railway station is Satna Junction (78 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pandav Falls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pandav Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pandav Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Pandav Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pandav Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gatha Falls, Jaruapur.
